mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer spending, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE increase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher equity valuations over the next month during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 50% of cases when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting sustained corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter GDP growth exceeding 2.0% signals strong aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during recovery phases.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indices indicate weakening manufacturing activity and reduced business investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum correlates with positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations.
